content.problemStatement:
A certain mountain range has NNN mountains, all along a line. The peak of the iii-th mountain is at a height of HiH_iHi‚Äã.

It is known that adjacent mountain peaks differ in height by exactly 111, i.e. ‚à£Hi‚àíHi‚àí1‚à£=1|H_i - H_{i-1}| = 1‚à£Hi‚Äã‚àíHi‚àí1‚Äã‚à£=1 for every iii.

On each mountain peak lives a single person.

A person can move from one mountain peak to an adjacent mountain peak (i.e. a person standing on the iii-th peak can move to i‚àí1i-1i‚àí1 or i+1i+1i+1).

However, they will refuse to move to a mountain that's of the same height as one that has been visited before.

For example, if the heights are [1,2,3,2,3][1, 2, 3, 2, 3][1,2,3,2,3], the person who is initially on the first peak can move to the second and third peaks, but will not move to the fourth (since it has a height of 222, which has already been visited along the way).

Similarly, the person initially on the fourth peak can move to the third or the fifth peak, but will not move to the second peak, since it has a height of 222 which is the same as the starting height.

Two people are considered to have a friendship between them if they can both reach the same mountain via some sequence of moves.

In the above example of [1,2,3,2,3][1, 2, 3, 2, 3][1,2,3,2,3], the people on mountains 222 and 333 are friends (person 222 can move to mountain 333), and the people on mountains 111 and 444 are also friends (they can both move to mountain 333). However, the people on mountains 111 and 555 are not friends, because there's no common mountain where they can meet.

Given the heights of the mountain peaks, find the number of friendships.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN  ‚Äî the number of mountain peaks.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers H1,H2,‚Ä¶,HNH_1, H_2, \ldots, H_NH1‚Äã,H2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,HN‚Äã, describing the mountain heights.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single integer: the number of friendships.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Every person is able to reach the highest mountain, i.e. 555. Since for every pair of people there's a mountain they can both reach, every pair of people will be friends.
Test case 222: The friendships are as follows:

Persons 111 and 222 are friends: they're already adjacent to each other so one of them can just move to the other.
Similarly (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) and (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4) are friends.
Persons 111 and 333 are friends: they can both move to mountain 222 and meet there.
Similarly, persons 222 and 444 are friends, since they can meet at mountain 333.
However, there's no way for persons 111 and 444 to ever meet.

The total number of friendships is hence 555.
